rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BISHOP COHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash.
rulings.Rulings: Respondent Mary E. Cohen's motion to quash is denied. Respondent waived personal jurisdiction on two separate grounds: (1) her motion to quash was untimely and (2) Ms. Cohen appeared generally by arguing the substantive merits of the underlying petition. Petitioner properly served Ms. Cohen. The proof of service filed by petitioner established a rebuttable presumption of proper service that Ms. Cohen has not rebutted. The prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 7/14/2015, at 4:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHIEKO CUZZORT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel John Reed To Answer Questions At Deposition And For Order Imposing Monetary And Terminating Sanctions Against John Reed, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Therof
rulings.Rulings: The motion to compel Mr. Reed to appear for deposition and for sanctions is granted, no opposition having been filed. The notice of deposition appears procedurally proper, Mr. Reed did not appear, and Mr. Reed has suggested no reason why the deposition should not be compelled. Pursuant to CCP section 2025.450(g), the Court imposes sanctions on Mr. Reed in the amount of $1035. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the opposing party and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, July 14, 2015.
